#2 Privilege Escalation - Unprotected Admin Endpoint Other

critical ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/admin/
Parameter: endpoint
Payload:
/admin/
Evidence:
Admin endpoint accessible without authentication (HTTP 200)
Description:

Administrative endpoint /admin/ is accessible without proper authentication

AI Analysis (Confidence: 75%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A01:2025 - Broken Access Control

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Critical - May allow unauthorized administrative access

CWE: CWE-269

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement proper role-based access control (RBAC) on the server side

  2. Never trust client-supplied role or privilege information

  3. Validate user permissions on every request server-side

  4. Use secure session management to track user roles

  5. Implement the principle of least privilege

  6. Log and monitor privilege escalation attempts

  7. Protect administrative endpoints with strong authentication



Example (Python/Flask):
```python
from functools import wraps
from flask import session, abort

def admin_required(f):
@wraps(f)
def decorated_function(*args, **kwargs):
# Always check role from server-side session/database
user = get_user_from_session(session['user_id'])
if not user or user.role != 'admin':
abort(403) # Forbidden
return f(*args, **kwargs)
return decorated_function

@app.route('/admin/dashboard')
@admin_required
def admin_dashboard():
return render_template('admin/dashboard.html')

<!-- InstanceBeginEditable name="content_rgn" -->
<div id="content">
	Error: You have an error in your SQL syntax; check the manual that corresponds to your MySQL server version for the right syntax to use near ''' at line 1</div></div></body></html>
Description:

SQL Injection vulnerability detected using Error-based technique. Database identified as MySQL.
An attacker can exploit this vulnerability to:
- Extract sensitive data from the database (usernames, passwords, personal information)
- Modify or delete database records
- Bypass authentication mechanisms
- Potentially execute operating system commands (depending on database configuration)
- Escalate privileges within the application


AI Analysis (Confidence: 90%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A05:2025 - Injection

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Critical - May allow database extraction or modification

CWE: CWE-89

💡 Remediation

▼
## Remediation Steps

### 1. Use Parameterized Queries (Prepared Statements)
This is the primary defense against SQL injection.

Python (SQLite/MySQL):
# VULNERABLE
cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {user_id}")

# SECURE
cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?", (user_id,))

Node.js:

// VULNERABLE
db.query(SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${userId});

// SECURE
db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [userId]);


  1. Use ORM Libraries


Object-Relational Mappers handle parameterization automatically:
Python: SQLAlchemy, Django ORM
Node.js: Sequelize, TypeORM
Java: Hibernate, JPA

  1. Input Validation


Validate input type, length, format, and range
Use allowlists for expected values
Reject unexpected or malformed input

  1. Least Privilege


Use database accounts with minimal required permissions
Avoid using 'root' or 'sa' accounts for application connections
Restrict access to sensitive tables and procedures

  1. Web Application Firewall (WAF)


Deploy WAF rules to detect/block SQL injection patterns
Use as defense-in-depth, not primary protection

  1. Error Handling


Never expose database errors to users
Log detailed errors server-side only
Return generic error messages to clients

    document.MM_pgW=innerWidth; document.MM_pgH=innerHeight; onresize=MM_reloadPage; }}
  else if (innerWidth!=document.MM_pgW || innerHeight!=document.MM_pgH) location.reload();
}

<div id="content">
	Error: You have an error in your SQL syntax; check the manual that corresponds to your MySQL server version for the right syntax to use near ''' at line 1</div></div></body></html>
Description:

SQL Injection vulnerability detected using Error-based technique. Database identified as MySQL.
An attacker can exploit this vulnerability to:
- Extract sensitive data from the database (usernames, passwords, personal information)
- Modify or delete database records
- Bypass authentication mechanisms
- Potentially execute operating system commands (depending on database configuration)
- Escalate privileges within the application


AI Analysis (Confidence: 90%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A05:2025 - Injection

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Critical - May allow database extraction or modification

CWE: CWE-89

💡 Remediation

▼
## Remediation Steps

### 1. Use Parameterized Queries (Prepared Statements)
This is the primary defense against SQL injection.

Python (SQLite/MySQL):
# VULNERABLE
cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {user_id}")

# SECURE
cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?", (user_id,))

Node.js:

// VULNERABLE
db.query(SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${userId});

// SECURE
db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [userId]);


  1. Use ORM Libraries


Object-Relational Mappers handle parameterization automatically:
Python: SQLAlchemy, Django ORM
Node.js: Sequelize, TypeORM
Java: Hibernate, JPA

  1. Input Validation


Validate input type, length, format, and range
Use allowlists for expected values
Reject unexpected or malformed input

  1. Least Privilege


Use database accounts with minimal required permissions
Avoid using 'root' or 'sa' accounts for application connections
Restrict access to sensitive tables and procedures

  1. Web Application Firewall (WAF)


Deploy WAF rules to detect/block SQL injection patterns
Use as defense-in-depth, not primary protection

  1. Error Handling


Never expose database errors to users
Log detailed errors server-side only
Return generic error messages to clients

#29 SQL Injection (Error-based) A05

critical ▼
🔖
A05: Injection
SQL, NoSQL, OS command, LDAP, XPath injection and XSS. Untrusted data sent to interpreters.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/product.php
Parameter: pic
Payload:
'
Evidence:
SQL Error detected: ...v id="content"> Error: You have an error in your SQL syntax; check the manual that corresponds to your MySQL server version for the right syntax to use near ''' at line 1</div></div></body></html>...

server: nginx/1.19.0

<div id="content">
	Error: You have an error in your SQL syntax; check the manual that corresponds to your MySQL server version for the right syntax to use near ''' at line 1</div></div></body></html>
Description:

SQL Injection vulnerability detected using Error-based technique. Database identified as MySQL.
An attacker can exploit this vulnerability to:
- Extract sensitive data from the database (usernames, passwords, personal information)
- Modify or delete database records
- Bypass authentication mechanisms
- Potentially execute operating system commands (depending on database configuration)
- Escalate privileges within the application


AI Analysis (Confidence: 90%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A05:2025 - Injection

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Critical - May allow database extraction or modification

CWE: CWE-89

💡 Remediation

▼
## Remediation Steps

### 1. Use Parameterized Queries (Prepared Statements)
This is the primary defense against SQL injection.

Python (SQLite/MySQL):
# VULNERABLE
cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {user_id}")

# SECURE
cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?", (user_id,))

Node.js:

// VULNERABLE
db.query(SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${userId});

// SECURE
db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [userId]);


  1. Use ORM Libraries


Object-Relational Mappers handle parameterization automatically:
Python: SQLAlchemy, Django ORM
Node.js: Sequelize, TypeORM
Java: Hibernate, JPA

  1. Input Validation


Validate input type, length, format, and range
Use allowlists for expected values
Reject unexpected or malformed input

  1. Least Privilege


Use database accounts with minimal required permissions
Avoid using 'root' or 'sa' accounts for application connections
Restrict access to sensitive tables and procedures

  1. Web Application Firewall (WAF)


Deploy WAF rules to detect/block SQL injection patterns
Use as defense-in-depth, not primary protection

  1. Error Handling


Never expose database errors to users
Log detailed errors server-side only
Return generic error messages to clients

#30 SQL Injection (Authentication Bypass) A05

critical ▼
🔖
A05: Injection
SQL, NoSQL, OS command, LDAP, XPath injection and XSS. Untrusted data sent to interpreters.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/login.php
Parameter: uname
Payload:
' OR '1'='1
Evidence:
Login bypassed successfully - redirected to user area

Host: testphp.vulnweb.com

AI Analysis (Confidence: 85%):

💡 Remediation

▼

### 1. Use Parameterized Queries (Prepared Statements)
This is the primary defense against SQL injection.

Python (SQLite/MySQL):
# VULNERABLE

# SECURE
cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?", (user_id,))

Node.js:

// VULNERABLE
db.query(SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${userId});

// SECURE
db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [userId]);


  1. Use ORM Libraries


Object-Relational Mappers handle parameterization automatically:
Python: SQLAlchemy, Django ORM

  1. Input Validation


Validate input type, length, format, and range

Avoid using 'root' or 'sa' accounts for application connections

#31 Vulnerable nginx Version A03

high ▼
🔖
A03: Software Supply Chain Failures
Vulnerabilities in dependencies, CI/CD pipelines, and software components. Expanded beyond just outdated components.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Parameter: Server
Payload:
nginx/1.19.0
Evidence:
Detected nginx 1.19.0 (vulnerable <= 1.20.0)
Description:

DNS resolver vulnerability (CVE-2021-23017)

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1104

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Update nginx to the latest stable version immediately

  2. Subscribe to security mailing lists for nginx

  3. Implement a patch management process

  4. Consider using automated update tools where appropriate



For nginx:


  • nginx: Use package manager or compile from source (nginx.org)






  1. After updating, hide version information:



Apache (httpd.conf):
ServerTokens Prod
ServerSignature Off

nginx (nginx.conf):

server_tokens off;
IIS (web.config):


xml
<system.webServer>
<httpProtocol>
<customHeaders>
<remove name="X-Powered-By" />
</customHeaders>
</httpProtocol>
</system.webServer>

#32 Vulnerable PHP Version A03

high ▼
🔖
A03: Software Supply Chain Failures
Vulnerabilities in dependencies, CI/CD pipelines, and software components. Expanded beyond just outdated components.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Parameter: X-Powered-By
Payload:
PHP/5.6.40-38+ubuntu20.04.1+deb.sury.org+1
Evidence:
Detected PHP 5.6.40 (vulnerable <= 7.4.29)
Description:

Buffer overflow vulnerability (CVE-2022-31626)

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1104

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Update PHP to the latest stable version immediately

  2. Subscribe to security mailing lists for PHP

  3. Implement a patch management process

  4. Consider using automated update tools where appropriate



For PHP:




  • PHP: Use package manager or download from php.net




  1. After updating, hide version information:



Apache (httpd.conf):
ServerTokens Prod
ServerSignature Off

nginx (nginx.conf):

server_tokens off;
IIS (web.config):


xml
<system.webServer>
<httpProtocol>
<customHeaders>
<remove name="X-Powered-By" />
</customHeaders>
</httpProtocol>
</system.webServer>

#33 End-of-Life PHP Version A03

high ▼
🔖
A03: Software Supply Chain Failures
Vulnerabilities in dependencies, CI/CD pipelines, and software components. Expanded beyond just outdated components.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Parameter: X-Powered-By
Payload:
PHP/5.6.40-38+ubuntu20.04.1+deb.sury.org+1
Evidence:
PHP 5.6.40 is end-of-life and no longer receives security updates
Description:

Server is running PHP 5.6.40 which has reached end-of-life

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1104

💡 Remediation

▼
⚠️ CRITICAL: PHP 5.6.40 has reached End-of-Life!

Upgrade PHP immediately to a supported version:

PHP 8.1 (Security support until Nov 2024)
PHP 8.2 (Security support until Dec 2025)
PHP 8.3 (Active support)
Before upgrading:

Test application compatibility
Review PHP migration guides
Update deprecated function calls
Upgrade commands:

Ubuntu/Debian:

bash
sudo apt update
sudo apt install php8.2
sudo a2dismod php5.6
sudo a2enmod php8.2
sudo systemctl restart apache2
CentOS/RHEL (with Remi):

bash
sudo dnf module reset php
sudo dnf module enable php:remi-8.2
sudo dnf install php
Hide PHP version after upgrade:
ini
; php.ini
expose_php = Off

#34 Sensitive Path Exposed: Admin Panel A01

high ▼
🔖
A01: Broken Access Control
Failures in access control enforcement, allowing users to act outside intended permissions. Now includes SSRF.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/admin/
Evidence:
HTTP 200 - Admin Panel accessible at admin/
Description:

The sensitive resource 'admin/' is publicly accessible. This could expose sensitive information or functionality.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-425

💡 Remediation

▼
Restrict access to sensitive paths using proper authentication and authorization. Consider removing unnecessary files from production servers.

#35 CSRF - Missing Token in Form A01

high ▼
🔖
A01: Broken Access Control
Failures in access control enforcement, allowing users to act outside intended permissions. Now includes SSRF.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/search.php?test=query
Parameter: form
Payload:
Method: POST, Action: search.php?test=query
Evidence:
Form without CSRF token found: <form action="search.php?test=query" method="post"> <label>search art</label> <input name="searchFor" size="10" type="text"/> <input name="goButton" type="submit" value="go"/> </form>...
Description:

A form that performs state-changing operations does not include a CSRF token, making it vulnerable to CSRF attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 75%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-352

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement anti-CSRF tokens (synchronizer token pattern)

  2. Use the Double Submit Cookie pattern as an alternative

  3. Set SameSite=Strict or SameSite=Lax on session cookies

  4. Verify the Origin and Referer headers for state-changing requests

  5. For APIs, require custom headers (e.g., X-Requested-With)

  6. Use frameworks' built-in CSRF protection


Example (Python/Flask):
from flask_wtf.csrf import CSRFProtect
csrf = CSRFProtect(app)
# In templates:
<form method="post">
    {{ csrf_token() }}
    ...
</form>

Example (Django):
# In templates:
<form method="post">
    {% csrf_token %}
    ...
</form>

#36 CRLF Header Injection A05

high ▼
🔖
A05: Injection
SQL, NoSQL, OS command, LDAP, XPath injection and XSS. Untrusted data sent to interpreters.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Parameter: test
Payload:
test Content-Type: text/html <script>alert('XSS')</script>
Evidence:
=== RESPONSE HEADERS === Server: nginx/1.19.0 Date: Tue, 24 Feb 2026 19:56:36 GMT >>>>> INJECTED HEADER START >>>>> Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8 <<<<< INJECTED HEADER END <<<<< Transfer-Encoding: chunked Connection: keep-alive X-Powered-By: PHP/5.6.40-38+ubuntu20.04.1+deb.sury.org+1 Content-Encoding: gzip === INJECTION DETAILS === Injection Point: test Payload Sent: "test\r\nContent-Type: text/html\r\n\r\n<script>alert('XSS')</script>" Injected Header: content-type Total Headers in Response: 7
Description:

CRLF injection allows arbitrary header injection via Standard CRLF

AI Analysis (Confidence: 75%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-113

💡 Remediation

▼
CRLF Injection Prevention:

  1. Input Validation: Reject or encode CR (\r, %0d) and LF (\n, %0a) characters

  2. Output Encoding: Properly encode data before including in HTTP headers

  3. Framework Protection: Use modern frameworks that auto-escape header values

  4. Allowlist Validation: For redirect URLs, validate against an allowlist

  5. URL Encoding: Properly URL-encode user input used in headers


Example (Python):
import re

def sanitize_header_value(value):
    # Remove CRLF sequences
    return re.sub(r'[\r\n]', '', value)

def safe_redirect(url):
    # Validate URL and remove CRLF
    if not url.startswith(('http://', 'https://')):
        raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme")
    return sanitize_header_value(url)

Example (Java):
// Use ESAPI or similar library
String safeValue = ESAPI.encoder().encodeForHTTPHeader(userInput);

  1. Input Validation: Reject or encode CR (\r, %0d) and LF (\n, %0a) characters

  2. Output Encoding: Properly encode data before including in HTTP headers

  3. Framework Protection: Use modern frameworks that auto-escape header values

  4. Allowlist Validation: For redirect URLs, validate against an allowlist

  5. URL Encoding: Properly URL-encode user input used in headers


Example (Python):
import re

def sanitize_header_value(value):
    # Remove CRLF sequences
    return re.sub(r'[\r\n]', '', value)

def safe_redirect(url):
    # Validate URL and remove CRLF
    if not url.startswith(('http://', 'https://')):
        raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme")
    return sanitize_header_value(url)

Example (Java):
// Use ESAPI or similar library
String safeValue = ESAPI.encoder().encodeForHTTPHeader(userInput);

#40 CSRF - Missing Token in Form A01

high ▼
🔖
A01: Broken Access Control
Failures in access control enforcement, allowing users to act outside intended permissions. Now includes SSRF.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/userinfo.php
Parameter: form
Payload:
Method: POST, Action: userinfo.php
Evidence:
Form without CSRF token found: <form action="userinfo.php" method="post" name="loginform"> <table cellpadding="4" cellspacing="1"> <tr><td>Username : </td><td><input name="uname" size="20" style="width:120px;" type="text"/></td></t...
Description:

A form that performs state-changing operations does not include a CSRF token, making it vulnerable to CSRF attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 75%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-352

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement anti-CSRF tokens (synchronizer token pattern)

  2. Use the Double Submit Cookie pattern as an alternative

  3. Set SameSite=Strict or SameSite=Lax on session cookies

  4. Verify the Origin and Referer headers for state-changing requests

  5. For APIs, require custom headers (e.g., X-Requested-With)

  6. Use frameworks' built-in CSRF protection


Example (Python/Flask):
from flask_wtf.csrf import CSRFProtect
csrf = CSRFProtect(app)
# In templates:
<form method="post">
    {{ csrf_token() }}
    ...
</form>

Example (Django):
# In templates:
<form method="post">
    {% csrf_token %}
    ...
</form>

  4. Allowlist Validation: For redirect URLs, validate against an allowlist

#42 Brute Force - Missing Rate Limiting Other

high ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/login.php
Parameter: login endpoint
Payload:
10 requests in 2.05s
Evidence:
All 10 login attempts accepted without rate limiting
Description:

Login endpoint lacks rate limiting, allowing unlimited authentication attempts

AI Analysis (Confidence: 65%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow brute force or DoS attacks

CWE: CWE-307

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement rate limiting on authentication endpoints

  2. Use exponential backoff for repeated failures

  3. Consider CAPTCHA after several failed attempts

  4. Implement IP-based and account-based rate limiting



Example (Node.js with express-rate-limit):
```javascript
const rateLimit = require('express-rate-limit');

const loginLimiter = rateLimit({
windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, // 15 minutes
max: 5, // 5 attempts per window
message: 'Too many login attempts, please try again later'
});

app.post('/login', loginLimiter, (req, res) => {
// Login logic
});

#43 Sensitive Data Exposure: Password visible in error response A09

high ▼
🔖
A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to detect and respond to attacks.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/login.php
Evidence:
Found in normal response: password" size
Description:

Password visible in error response. Sensitive information was detected in the application response. This data may also be written to application logs, potentially exposing it to:
- Unauthorized staff with log access
- Attackers who compromise log storage
- Third-party log aggregation services
- Backup systems containing logs

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A04:2025 - Cryptographic Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: High - Direct data breach risk

CWE: CWE-532

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Never log sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII, credentials)

  2. Implement log data masking/redaction for sensitive fields

  3. Configure error handling to show generic messages to users

  4. Use separate detailed logging for development only

  5. Implement log data classification and retention policies

  6. Review and sanitize all error messages before production

#44 Sensitive Data Exposure: Password visible in error response A09

high ▼
🔖
A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to detect and respond to attacks.

Parameter: test
Payload:
{{invalid}}
Evidence:

Description:

Password visible in error response. Sensitive information was detected in the application response. This data may also be written to application logs, potentially exposing it to:

- Third-party log aggregation services
- Backup systems containing logs

▼

  1. Never log sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII, credentials)

  2. Implement log data masking/redaction for sensitive fields

  3. Configure error handling to show generic messages to users

  4. Use separate detailed logging for development only

  5. Implement log data classification and retention policies

  6. Review and sanitize all error messages before production

    return sanitize_header_value(url)

high ▼

=== RESPONSE HEADERS === Server: nginx/1.19.0 Date: Tue, 24 Feb 2026 19:57:10 GMT >>>>> INJECTED HEADER START >>>>> Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8 <<<<< INJECTED HEADER END <<<<< Transfer-Encoding: chunked Connection: keep-alive X-Powered-By: PHP/5.6.40-38+ubuntu20.04.1+deb.sury.org+1 Content-Encoding: gzip === INJECTION DETAILS === Injection Point: test Payload Sent: "test\r\nContent-Type: text/html\r\n\r\n<script>alert('XSS')</script>" Injected Header: content-type Total Headers in Response: 7

CRLF injection allows arbitrary header injection via Standard CRLF

AI Analysis (Confidence: 75%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-113

💡 Remediation

▼
CRLF Injection Prevention:

  1. Input Validation: Reject or encode CR (\r, %0d) and LF (\n, %0a) characters

  2. Output Encoding: Properly encode data before including in HTTP headers

  3. Framework Protection: Use modern frameworks that auto-escape header values

  4. Allowlist Validation: For redirect URLs, validate against an allowlist

  5. URL Encoding: Properly URL-encode user input used in headers


Example (Python):
import re

def sanitize_header_value(value):
    # Remove CRLF sequences
    return re.sub(r'[\r\n]', '', value)

def safe_redirect(url):
    # Validate URL and remove CRLF
    if not url.startswith(('http://', 'https://')):
        raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme")
    return sanitize_header_value(url)

#59 CSRF - Missing Token in Form A01

high ▼
🔖
A01: Broken Access Control
Failures in access control enforcement, allowing users to act outside intended permissions. Now includes SSRF.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/guestbook.php
Parameter: form
Payload:
Method: POST, Action: same page
Evidence:
Form without CSRF token found: <form action="" method="post" name="faddentry"> <input name="name" type="hidden" value="anonymous user"/> <textarea name="text" rows="5" style="width:500px;" wrap="VIRTUAL"></textarea> <br/> <input na...
Description:

A form that performs state-changing operations does not include a CSRF token, making it vulnerable to CSRF attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 75%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-352

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement anti-CSRF tokens (synchronizer token pattern)

  2. Use the Double Submit Cookie pattern as an alternative

  3. Set SameSite=Strict or SameSite=Lax on session cookies

  4. Verify the Origin and Referer headers for state-changing requests

  5. For APIs, require custom headers (e.g., X-Requested-With)

  6. Use frameworks' built-in CSRF protection


Example (Python/Flask):
from flask_wtf.csrf import CSRFProtect
csrf = CSRFProtect(app)
# In templates:
<form method="post">
    {{ csrf_token() }}
    ...
</form>

Example (Django):
# In templates:
<form method="post">
    {% csrf_token %}
    ...
</form>

#62 Brute Force - Missing Rate Limiting Other

high ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/userinfo.php
Parameter: login endpoint
Payload:
10 requests in 2.02s
Evidence:
All 10 login attempts accepted without rate limiting
Description:

Login endpoint lacks rate limiting, allowing unlimited authentication attempts

AI Analysis (Confidence: 65%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow brute force or DoS attacks

CWE: CWE-307

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement rate limiting on authentication endpoints

  2. Use exponential backoff for repeated failures

  3. Consider CAPTCHA after several failed attempts

  4. Implement IP-based and account-based rate limiting



Example (Node.js with express-rate-limit):
```javascript
const rateLimit = require('express-rate-limit');

const loginLimiter = rateLimit({
windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, // 15 minutes
max: 5, // 5 attempts per window
message: 'Too many login attempts, please try again later'
});

app.post('/login', loginLimiter, (req, res) => {
// Login logic
});

#63 Sensitive Data Exposure: Password visible in error response A09

high ▼
🔖
A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to detect and respond to attacks.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/userinfo.php
Evidence:
Found in normal response: password" size
Description:

Password visible in error response. Sensitive information was detected in the application response. This data may also be written to application logs, potentially exposing it to:
- Unauthorized staff with log access
- Attackers who compromise log storage
- Third-party log aggregation services
- Backup systems containing logs

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A04:2025 - Cryptographic Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: High - Direct data breach risk

CWE: CWE-532

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Never log sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII, credentials)

  2. Implement log data masking/redaction for sensitive fields

  3. Configure error handling to show generic messages to users

  4. Use separate detailed logging for development only

  5. Implement log data classification and retention policies

  6. Review and sanitize all error messages before production

  6. Review and sanitize all error messages before production

- Third-party log aggregation services
- Backup systems containing logs

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A04:2025 - Cryptographic Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: High - Direct data breach risk

CWE: CWE-532

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Never log sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII, credentials)

  2. Implement log data masking/redaction for sensitive fields

  3. Configure error handling to show generic messages to users

  4. Use separate detailed logging for development only

  5. Implement log data classification and retention policies

  6. Review and sanitize all error messages before production

#70 CRLF Header Injection A05

high ▼
🔖
A05: Injection
SQL, NoSQL, OS command, LDAP, XPath injection and XSS. Untrusted data sent to interpreters.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/hpp/
Parameter: test
Payload:
test Content-Type: text/html <script>alert('XSS')</script>
Evidence:
=== RESPONSE HEADERS === Server: nginx/1.19.0 Date: Tue, 24 Feb 2026 19:57:35 GMT >>>>> INJECTED HEADER START >>>>> Content-Type: text/html; charset=UTF-8 <<<<< INJECTED HEADER END <<<<< Transfer-Encoding: chunked Connection: keep-alive X-Powered-By: PHP/5.6.40-38+ubuntu20.04.1+deb.sury.org+1 Content-Encoding: gzip === INJECTION DETAILS === Injection Point: test Payload Sent: "test\r\nContent-Type: text/html\r\n\r\n<script>alert('XSS')</script>" Injected Header: content-type Total Headers in Response: 7
Description:

CRLF injection allows arbitrary header injection via Standard CRLF

AI Analysis (Confidence: 75%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-113

💡 Remediation

▼
CRLF Injection Prevention:

  1. Input Validation: Reject or encode CR (\r, %0d) and LF (\n, %0a) characters

  2. Output Encoding: Properly encode data before including in HTTP headers

  3. Framework Protection: Use modern frameworks that auto-escape header values

  4. Allowlist Validation: For redirect URLs, validate against an allowlist

  5. URL Encoding: Properly URL-encode user input used in headers


Example (Python):
import re

def sanitize_header_value(value):
    # Remove CRLF sequences
    return re.sub(r'[\r\n]', '', value)

def safe_redirect(url):
    # Validate URL and remove CRLF
    if not url.startswith(('http://', 'https://')):
        raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme")
    return sanitize_header_value(url)

Example (Java):
// Use ESAPI or similar library
String safeValue = ESAPI.encoder().encodeForHTTPHeader(userInput);

#71 CSRF - Missing Token in Form A01

high ▼
🔖
A01: Broken Access Control
Failures in access control enforcement, allowing users to act outside intended permissions. Now includes SSRF.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/cart.php
Parameter: form
Payload:
Method: POST, Action: cart.php
Evidence:
Form without CSRF token found: <form action="cart.php" method="POST" name="f_addcart"><input name="price" type="hidden" value="1000"/><input name="addcart" type="hidden" value="4"/><input type="submit" value="add this picture to ca...
Description:

A form that performs state-changing operations does not include a CSRF token, making it vulnerable to CSRF attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 75%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-352

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement anti-CSRF tokens (synchronizer token pattern)

  2. Use the Double Submit Cookie pattern as an alternative

  3. Set SameSite=Strict or SameSite=Lax on session cookies

  4. Verify the Origin and Referer headers for state-changing requests

  5. For APIs, require custom headers (e.g., X-Requested-With)

  6. Use frameworks' built-in CSRF protection


Example (Python/Flask):
from flask_wtf.csrf import CSRFProtect
csrf = CSRFProtect(app)
# In templates:
<form method="post">
    {{ csrf_token() }}
    ...
</form>

Example (Django):
# In templates:
<form method="post">
    {% csrf_token %}
    ...
</form>

#72 Brute Force - Missing Rate Limiting Other

high ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/userinfo.php
Parameter: login endpoint
Payload:
10 requests in 2.00s
Evidence:
All 10 login attempts accepted without rate limiting
Description:

Login endpoint lacks rate limiting, allowing unlimited authentication attempts

AI Analysis (Confidence: 65%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow brute force or DoS attacks

CWE: CWE-307

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement rate limiting on authentication endpoints

  2. Use exponential backoff for repeated failures

  3. Consider CAPTCHA after several failed attempts

  4. Implement IP-based and account-based rate limiting



Example (Node.js with express-rate-limit):
```javascript
const rateLimit = require('express-rate-limit');

const loginLimiter = rateLimit({
windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, // 15 minutes
max: 5, // 5 attempts per window
message: 'Too many login attempts, please try again later'
});

app.post('/login', loginLimiter, (req, res) => {
// Login logic
});

#73 Brute Force - Missing Rate Limiting Other

high ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/userinfo.php
Parameter: login endpoint
Payload:
10 requests in 2.01s
Evidence:
All 10 login attempts accepted without rate limiting
Description:

Login endpoint lacks rate limiting, allowing unlimited authentication attempts

AI Analysis (Confidence: 65%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow brute force or DoS attacks

CWE: CWE-307

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement rate limiting on authentication endpoints

  2. Use exponential backoff for repeated failures

  3. Consider CAPTCHA after several failed attempts

  4. Implement IP-based and account-based rate limiting



Example (Node.js with express-rate-limit):
```javascript
const rateLimit = require('express-rate-limit');

const loginLimiter = rateLimit({
windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, // 15 minutes
max: 5, // 5 attempts per window
message: 'Too many login attempts, please try again later'
});

app.post('/login', loginLimiter, (req, res) => {
// Login logic
});

#74 Cross-Site Scripting (Form - Alert Dialog (Form Submit)) A05

high ▼
🔖
A05: Injection
SQL, NoSQL, OS command, LDAP, XPath injection and XSS. Untrusted data sent to interpreters.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/search.php?test=query
Parameter: searchFor
Payload:
<iframe src="javascript:alert(`VULNFLOW_XSS_5ac43c5e_JS1`)">
Evidence:
XSS confirmed in form input. Payload type: juiceshop_dom_xss_iframe
Description:

Confirmed XSS vulnerability in form submission. Malicious JavaScript executes when the form is submitted.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 80%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A05:2025 - Injection

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: High - May allow session hijacking or data theft

CWE: CWE-79

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Output Encoding: Encode all user input before rendering:



  • HTML context: Encode < > & " '

  • JavaScript context: Use JSON.stringify()

  • URL context: Use encodeURIComponent()




  1. Content Security Policy: Implement strict CSP headers:


Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'


  1. Use Framework Protections:



  • React: JSX auto-escapes by default

  • Angular: Uses contextual auto-escaping

  • Vue: Use v-text instead of v-html




  1. Input Validation: Validate and sanitize input server-side




  1. HTTPOnly Cookies: Prevent cookie theft via XSS




  1. Use DOMPurify: For any HTML that must be rendered from user input


const clean = DOMPurify.sanitize(dirty);

#75 Cross-Site Scripting (Form - Alert Dialog (Form Submit)) A05

high ▼
🔖
A05: Injection
SQL, NoSQL, OS command, LDAP, XPath injection and XSS. Untrusted data sent to interpreters.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/guestbook.php
Parameter: text
Payload:

	<td align="left">
		<a href="index.php">home</a> | <a href="categories.php">categories</a> | <a href="artists.php">artists
		</a> | <a href="disclaimer.php">disclaimer</a> | <a href="cart.php">your cart</a> | 
		<a href="guestbook.php">guestbook</a> | 
		<a href="AJAX/index.php">AJAX Demo</a>

<!-- end masthead --> 

<!-- begin content -->
<!-- InstanceBeginEditable name="content_rgn" -->
<div id="content">
	Error: You have an error in your SQL syntax; check the manual that corresponds to your MySQL server version for the right syntax to use near ''' at line 1</div></div></body></html>
Description:

SQL Injection vulnerability detected using Error-based technique. Database identified as MySQL.
An attacker can exploit this vulnerability to:
- Extract sensitive data from the database (usernames, passwords, personal information)
- Modify or delete database records
- Bypass authentication mechanisms
- Potentially execute operating system commands (depending on database configuration)
- Escalate privileges within the application


AI Analysis (Confidence: 90%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A05:2025 - Injection

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Critical - May allow database extraction or modification

CWE: CWE-89

💡 Remediation

▼
## Remediation Steps

### 1. Use Parameterized Queries (Prepared Statements)
This is the primary defense against SQL injection.

Python (SQLite/MySQL):
# VULNERABLE
cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {user_id}")

# SECURE
cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?", (user_id,))

Node.js:

// VULNERABLE
db.query(SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${userId});

// SECURE
db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [userId]);


  1. Use ORM Libraries


Object-Relational Mappers handle parameterization automatically:
Python: SQLAlchemy, Django ORM
Node.js: Sequelize, TypeORM
Java: Hibernate, JPA

  1. Input Validation


Validate input type, length, format, and range
Use allowlists for expected values
Reject unexpected or malformed input

  1. Least Privilege


Use database accounts with minimal required permissions
Avoid using 'root' or 'sa' accounts for application connections
Restrict access to sensitive tables and procedures

  1. Web Application Firewall (WAF)


Deploy WAF rules to detect/block SQL injection patterns
Use as defense-in-depth, not primary protection

  1. Error Handling


Never expose database errors to users
Log detailed errors server-side only
Return generic error messages to clients

Description:

SQL Injection vulnerability detected using Error-based technique. Database identified as MySQL.
An attacker can exploit this vulnerability to:
- Extract sensitive data from the database (usernames, passwords, personal information)
- Modify or delete database records
- Bypass authentication mechanisms
- Potentially execute operating system commands (depending on database configuration)
- Escalate privileges within the application


AI Analysis (Confidence: 90%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A05:2025 - Injection

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Critical - May allow database extraction or modification

CWE: CWE-89

💡 Remediation

▼
## Remediation Steps

### 1. Use Parameterized Queries (Prepared Statements)
This is the primary defense against SQL injection.

Python (SQLite/MySQL):
# VULNERABLE
cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {user_id}")

# SECURE
cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?", (user_id,))

Node.js:

// VULNERABLE
db.query(SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${userId});

// SECURE
db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [userId]);


  1. Use ORM Libraries


Object-Relational Mappers handle parameterization automatically:
Python: SQLAlchemy, Django ORM
Node.js: Sequelize, TypeORM
Java: Hibernate, JPA

  1. Input Validation


Validate input type, length, format, and range
Use allowlists for expected values
Reject unexpected or malformed input

  1. Least Privilege


Use database accounts with minimal required permissions
Avoid using 'root' or 'sa' accounts for application connections
Restrict access to sensitive tables and procedures

  1. Web Application Firewall (WAF)


Deploy WAF rules to detect/block SQL injection patterns
Use as defense-in-depth, not primary protection

  1. Error Handling


Never expose database errors to users
Log detailed errors server-side only
Return generic error messages to clients

<!-- begin content -->
<!-- InstanceBeginEditable name="content_rgn" -->
<div id="content">
	Error: You have an error in your SQL syntax; check the manual that corresponds to your MySQL server version for the right syntax to use near ''' at line 1</div></div></body></html>
Description:

SQL Injection vulnerability detected using Error-based technique. Database identified as MySQL.
An attacker can exploit this vulnerability to:
- Extract sensitive data from the database (usernames, passwords, personal information)
- Modify or delete database records
- Bypass authentication mechanisms
- Potentially execute operating system commands (depending on database configuration)
- Escalate privileges within the application


AI Analysis (Confidence: 90%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A05:2025 - Injection

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Critical - May allow database extraction or modification

CWE: CWE-89

💡 Remediation

▼
## Remediation Steps

### 1. Use Parameterized Queries (Prepared Statements)
This is the primary defense against SQL injection.

Python (SQLite/MySQL):
# VULNERABLE
cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {user_id}")

# SECURE
cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?", (user_id,))

Node.js:

// VULNERABLE
db.query(SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${userId});

// SECURE
db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [userId]);


  1. Use ORM Libraries


Object-Relational Mappers handle parameterization automatically:
Python: SQLAlchemy, Django ORM
Node.js: Sequelize, TypeORM
Java: Hibernate, JPA

  1. Input Validation


Validate input type, length, format, and range
Use allowlists for expected values
Reject unexpected or malformed input

  1. Least Privilege


Use database accounts with minimal required permissions
Avoid using 'root' or 'sa' accounts for application connections
Restrict access to sensitive tables and procedures

  1. Web Application Firewall (WAF)


Deploy WAF rules to detect/block SQL injection patterns
Use as defense-in-depth, not primary protection

  1. Error Handling


Never expose database errors to users
Log detailed errors server-side only
Return generic error messages to clients

#80 SQL Injection (Error-based) A05

high ▼
🔖
A05: Injection
SQL, NoSQL, OS command, LDAP, XPath injection and XSS. Untrusted data sent to interpreters.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/secured/newuser.php
Parameter: ucc
Payload:
' OR '1'='1
Evidence:
SQL Error detected: ...<html><head></head><body>Warning: mysql_connect(): Connection refused in /hj/var/www/database_connect.php on line 2 Website is out of order....
▶ 🔍 HTTP Traffic Click to view request/response
📤 Request 
POST /secured/newuser.php HTTP/1.1
Host: testphp.vulnweb.com

uuname=test123&upass=test123&upass2=test123&urname=test123&ucc=%27+OR+%271%27%3D%271&uemail=test123&uphone=test123&uaddress=test123&signup=test123
Description:

SQL injection in form input. Method: POST

AI Analysis (Confidence: 90%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A05:2025 - Injection

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Critical - May allow database extraction or modification

CWE: CWE-89

💡 Remediation

▼
## Remediation Steps

### 1. Use Parameterized Queries (Prepared Statements)
This is the primary defense against SQL injection.

Python (SQLite/MySQL):
# VULNERABLE
cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {user_id}")

# SECURE
cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?", (user_id,))

Node.js:

// VULNERABLE
db.query(SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${userId});

// SECURE
db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [userId]);


  1. Use ORM Libraries


Object-Relational Mappers handle parameterization automatically:
Python: SQLAlchemy, Django ORM
Node.js: Sequelize, TypeORM
Java: Hibernate, JPA

  1. Input Validation


Validate input type, length, format, and range
Use allowlists for expected values
Reject unexpected or malformed input

  1. Least Privilege


Use database accounts with minimal required permissions
Avoid using 'root' or 'sa' accounts for application connections
Restrict access to sensitive tables and procedures

  1. Web Application Firewall (WAF)


Deploy WAF rules to detect/block SQL injection patterns
Use as defense-in-depth, not primary protection

  1. Error Handling


Never expose database errors to users
Log detailed errors server-side only
Return generic error messages to clients

## Remediation Steps

### 1. Use Parameterized Queries (Prepared Statements)
This is the primary defense against SQL injection.

Python (SQLite/MySQL):
# VULNERABLE
cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {user_id}")

cursor.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?", (user_id,))

Node.js:

// VULNERABLE
db.query(SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${userId});

#82 Missing Security Header: Strict-Transport-Security A02

medium ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Evidence:
Header 'Strict-Transport-Security' is not present in the response
Description:

HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) not set. HSTS ensures browsers only connect via HTTPS, preventing SSL stripping attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 90%):
The absence of the Strict-Transport-Security header in the response indicates a potential vulnerability to man-in-the-middle attacks, as the application does not enforce HTTPS connections. This is a clear indication of a security misconfiguration. The lack of this header allows an attacker to intercept and modify traffic between the client and server.

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: An attacker could exploit this vulnerability to steal sensitive user data, such as session cookies or login credentials, by intercepting and modifying traffic between the client and server.

CWE: CWE-319

💡 Remediation

▼
Add the following header to your server configuration:
Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload

#83 Missing Security Header: Content-Security-Policy A02

medium ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Evidence:
Header 'Content-Security-Policy' is not present in the response
Description:

Content Security Policy (CSP) not set. CSP helps prevent XSS and data injection attacks by controlling resource loading.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 90%):
The absence of the Content-Security-Policy header in the response increases the risk of XSS attacks, as it allows an attacker to inject malicious scripts. This is a clear indication of a security misconfiguration. The lack of this header makes the application more vulnerable to attacks.

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: The absence of the Content-Security-Policy header can lead to significant business impact, including data breaches, financial loss, and reputational damage, if an attacker successfully exploits the vulnerability.

CWE: CWE-693

💡 Remediation

▼
Add the following header to your server configuration:
Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'

#84 Missing Security Header: X-Frame-Options A02

medium ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Evidence:
Header 'X-Frame-Options' is not present in the response
Description:

X-Frame-Options header not set. This header prevents clickjacking attacks by controlling iframe embedding.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 60%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A02:2025 - Security Misconfiguration

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Low - Increases attack surface

CWE: CWE-1021

💡 Remediation

▼
Add the following header to your server configuration:
X-Frame-Options: DENY or SAMEORIGIN

#85 Directory Listing Enabled Other

medium ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/images/
Parameter: Directory
Payload:
/images/
Evidence:
Directory listing is enabled
Description:

Directory listing enabled for /images/

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-548

💡 Remediation

▼
Disable directory listing in web server configuration.

#86 Missing Content Security Policy A03

medium ▼
🔖
A03: Software Supply Chain Failures
Vulnerabilities in dependencies, CI/CD pipelines, and software components. Expanded beyond just outdated components.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Parameter: header
Payload:
Content-Security-Policy
Evidence:
No CSP header present
Description:

Content Security Policy header is not configured, reducing protection against XSS and injection attacks

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-693

💡 Remediation

▼
Implement Content Security Policy:

Content-Security-Policy:
default-src 'self';
script-src 'self' https://trusted-cdn.com;
style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline';
img-src 'self' data: https:;
frame-ancestors 'none';

Avoid 'unsafe-inline' and 'unsafe-eval' where possible.
Use nonces or hashes for necessary inline scripts.

#87 Missing Clickjacking Protection A06

medium ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Parameter: X-Frame-Options
Payload:
N/A
Evidence:
Neither X-Frame-Options nor CSP frame-ancestors present
Description:

The page can be embedded in an iframe, potentially allowing clickjacking attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1021

💡 Remediation

▼
Clickjacking Prevention:


  1. Use X-Frame-Options Header


X-Frame-Options: DENY

or
X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN



  1. Use Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors (Recommended)


Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'self'

or
Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none'



  1. For Specific Trusted Origins



``Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'self' https://trusted.com


    

  1. Server Configuration Examples:
    2. 



Nginx:
nginx
add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
add_header Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self'" always;
Apache:
apache
Header always set X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN"
Header always set Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self'"
Express.js:
javascript
const helmet = require('helmet');
app.use(helmet.frameguard({ action: 'sameorigin' }));
``


  1. Avoid JavaScript Frame-Busting



  • JavaScript solutions can be bypassed

  • Use HTTP headers instead

#88 Debug Parameter Accepted A06

medium ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Parameter: debug
Payload:
debug=true
Evidence:
Response changed when debug parameter added
Description:

The application accepts debug parameter 'debug', potentially exposing sensitive information.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-489

💡 Remediation

▼
Remove or disable debug parameters in production.

#89 No MFA Implementation Detected A07

medium ▼
🔖
A07: Authentication Failures
Weaknesses in authentication, session management, credential handling, and identity verification.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Parameter: Authentication
Payload:
N/A
Evidence:
No MFA endpoints or indicators found
Description:

The application does not appear to implement multi-factor authentication.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A07:2025 - Authentication Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-308

💡 Remediation

▼
Multi-Factor Authentication Best Practices:


  1. Implement TOTP-Based MFA



  • Support Google Authenticator, Authy, Microsoft Authenticator

  • Use standard TOTP algorithm (RFC 6238)

  • Generate secure secrets (minimum 160 bits)




  1. Avoid SMS-Only MFA



  • SMS is vulnerable to SIM swapping

  • Offer SMS as backup only, not primary

  • Prefer app-based TOTP or hardware keys




  1. Support Hardware Security Keys



  • Implement WebAuthn/FIDO2

  • Support YubiKey and similar devices

  • Most secure MFA option




  1. Secure Recovery Codes



  • Generate cryptographically random codes

  • Hash stored recovery codes

  • Show codes only once during setup

  • Require re-authentication to regenerate




  1. Implementation Example (Python):


import pyotp
import secrets

def setup_totp(user):
    # Generate secure secret
    secret = pyotp.random_base32()
    
    # Store hashed secret
    user.totp_secret = hash_secret(secret)
    
    # Generate provisioning URI
    totp = pyotp.TOTP(secret)
    uri = totp.provisioning_uri(
        name=user.email,
        issuer_name="YourApp"
    )
    
    return uri, secret

def verify_totp(user, code):
    secret = get_secret(user)
    totp = pyotp.TOTP(secret)
    
    # Allow 1 window tolerance
    return totp.verify(code, valid_window=1)


  1. Enforce MFA for:



  • Admin accounts (mandatory)

  • Financial operations

  • Password changes

  • Security setting changes

  • API key management

#90 Session Fixation - Arbitrary Session Accepted Other

medium ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Parameter: PHPSESSID
Payload:
FAKEATTACKERSESSIONAAAAAAAAAAA...
Evidence:
Server did not reject arbitrary session ID
Description:

Application may accept arbitrary session identifiers

AI Analysis (Confidence: 75%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A07:2025 - Authentication Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: High - May allow session hijacking

CWE: CWE-384

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Regenerate session ID after successful authentication

  2. Regenerate session ID after privilege level change

  3. Don't accept session IDs from URL parameters

  4. Validate session IDs against expected format

  5. Use secure session management frameworks

  6. Implement session timeout and idle timeout



Example session regeneration:

  • PHP: session_regenerate_id(true)

  • Java: request.getSession().invalidate(); request.getSession(true)

  • .NET: SessionIDManager.CreateSessionID()

  • Python Flask: session.regenerate()

#96 Window.opener Vulnerability A06

medium ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/artists.php
Parameter: JavaScript
Payload:
.open('./comment.php?aid=1'
Evidence:
window.open() without noopener/noreferrer
Description:

window.open() is used without noopener, allowing the opened page to access window.opener.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1022

💡 Remediation

▼
Add 'noopener,noreferrer' to window.open() calls.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 75%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A07:2025 - Authentication Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: High - May allow session hijacking

CWE: CWE-384

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Regenerate session ID after successful authentication

  2. Regenerate session ID after privilege level change

  3. Don't accept session IDs from URL parameters

  4. Validate session IDs against expected format

  5. Use secure session management frameworks

  6. Implement session timeout and idle timeout



Example session regeneration:

  • PHP: session_regenerate_id(true)

  • Java: request.getSession().invalidate(); request.getSession(true)

  • .NET: SessionIDManager.CreateSessionID()

  • Python Flask: session.regenerate()

#99 Missing Clickjacking Protection A06

medium ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/cart.php
Parameter: X-Frame-Options
Payload:
N/A
Evidence:
Neither X-Frame-Options nor CSP frame-ancestors present
Description:

The page can be embedded in an iframe, potentially allowing clickjacking attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1021

💡 Remediation

▼
Clickjacking Prevention:


  1. Use X-Frame-Options Header


X-Frame-Options: DENY

or
X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN



  1. Use Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors (Recommended)


Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'self'

or
Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none'



  1. For Specific Trusted Origins



``Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'self' https://trusted.com


    

  1. Server Configuration Examples:
    2. 



Nginx:
nginx
add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
add_header Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self'" always;
Apache:
apache
Header always set X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN"
Header always set Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self'"
Express.js:
javascript
const helmet = require('helmet');
app.use(helmet.frameguard({ action: 'sameorigin' }));
``


  1. Avoid JavaScript Frame-Busting



  • JavaScript solutions can be bypassed

  • Use HTTP headers instead

#104 Brute Force - Missing Account Lockout Other

medium ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/login.php
Parameter: login endpoint
Payload:
15 failed attempts for same account
Evidence:
No account lockout detected after multiple failed attempts
Description:

Login endpoint does not implement account lockout after failed attempts

AI Analysis (Confidence: 60%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow credential compromise

CWE: CWE-307

💡 Remediation

▼
Implement account lockout after 5-10 failed attempts
Use progressive delays between attempts
Notify users of suspicious login activity
Provide secure account recovery mechanisms
Example policy:

Lock account for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts

Extend lockout duration with repeated lockouts

Send email notification on lockout

Allow unlock via email verification

#111 Insufficient Attack Signature Detection A09

medium ▼
🔖
A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to detect and respond to attacks.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/privacy.php
Evidence:
Only 0/18 attack signatures triggered blocking (0%)
Description:

The application shows minimal detection and blocking of known attack patterns. Common attack signatures for SQL injection, XSS, command injection, and path traversal were sent without triggering security responses.

Undetected attack types: SQL Injection, XSS

This indicates:
- No WAF or insufficient WAF rules
- Lack of attack detection and logging
- Unable to alert on active attacks in real-time

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-778

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Deploy a Web Application Firewall (WAF) with current rule sets

  2. Implement the OWASP ModSecurity Core Rule Set

  3. Configure logging for all blocked requests

  4. Set up real-time alerting for attack patterns

  5. Integrate with SIEM for correlation and analysis

  6. Regularly update attack signature databases

Nginx:
nginx
add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
add_header Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self'" always;
Apache:
apache
Header always set X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN"
Header always set Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self'"
Express.js:
javascript
const helmet = require('helmet');
app.use(helmet.frameguard({ action: 'sameorigin' }));
``


  1. Avoid JavaScript Frame-Busting



  • JavaScript solutions can be bypassed

  • Use HTTP headers instead

#122 Debug Parameter Accepted A06

medium ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/Mod_Rewrite_Shop/RateProduct-1.html
Parameter: debug
Payload:
debug=true
Evidence:
Response changed when debug parameter added
Description:

The application accepts debug parameter 'debug', potentially exposing sensitive information.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-489

💡 Remediation

▼
Remove or disable debug parameters in production.

#123 Insufficient Attack Signature Detection A09

medium ▼
🔖
A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to detect and respond to attacks.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/Mod_Rewrite_Shop/RateProduct-1.html
Evidence:
Only 0/18 attack signatures triggered blocking (0%)
Description:

The application shows minimal detection and blocking of known attack patterns. Common attack signatures for SQL injection, XSS, command injection, and path traversal were sent without triggering security responses.

Undetected attack types: SQL Injection, XSS

This indicates:
- No WAF or insufficient WAF rules
- Lack of attack detection and logging
- Unable to alert on active attacks in real-time

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-778

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Deploy a Web Application Firewall (WAF) with current rule sets

  2. Implement the OWASP ModSecurity Core Rule Set

  3. Configure logging for all blocked requests

  4. Set up real-time alerting for attack patterns

  5. Integrate with SIEM for correlation and analysis

  6. Regularly update attack signature databases

#124 Missing Clickjacking Protection A06

medium ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/Mod_Rewrite_Shop/BuyProduct-1/
Parameter: X-Frame-Options
Payload:
N/A
Evidence:
Neither X-Frame-Options nor CSP frame-ancestors present
Description:

The page can be embedded in an iframe, potentially allowing clickjacking attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1021

💡 Remediation

▼
Clickjacking Prevention:


  1. Use X-Frame-Options Header


X-Frame-Options: DENY

or
X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN



  1. Use Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors (Recommended)


Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'self'

or
Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none'



  1. For Specific Trusted Origins



``Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'self' https://trusted.com


    

  1. Server Configuration Examples:
    2. 



Nginx:
nginx
add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
add_header Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self'" always;
Apache:
apache
Header always set X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN"
Header always set Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self'"
Express.js:
javascript
const helmet = require('helmet');
app.use(helmet.frameguard({ action: 'sameorigin' }));
``


  1. Avoid JavaScript Frame-Busting



  • JavaScript solutions can be bypassed

  • Use HTTP headers instead

  6. Regularly update attack signature databases

#145 Missing Clickjacking Protection A06

medium ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/AJAX/index.php
Parameter: X-Frame-Options
Payload:
N/A
Evidence:
Neither X-Frame-Options nor CSP frame-ancestors present
Description:

The page can be embedded in an iframe, potentially allowing clickjacking attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1021

💡 Remediation

▼
Clickjacking Prevention:


  1. Use X-Frame-Options Header


X-Frame-Options: DENY

or
X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN



  1. Use Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors (Recommended)


Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'self'

or
Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none'



  1. For Specific Trusted Origins



``Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'self' https://trusted.com


    

  1. Server Configuration Examples:
    2. 



Nginx:
nginx
add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
add_header Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self'" always;
Apache:
apache
Header always set X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN"
Header always set Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self'"
Express.js:
javascript
const helmet = require('helmet');
app.use(helmet.frameguard({ action: 'sameorigin' }));
``


  1. Avoid JavaScript Frame-Busting



  • JavaScript solutions can be bypassed

  • Use HTTP headers instead

#146 Window.opener Vulnerability A06

medium ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/AJAX/index.php
Parameter: JavaScript
Payload:
.open('GET'
Evidence:
window.open() without noopener/noreferrer
Description:

window.open() is used without noopener, allowing the opened page to access window.opener.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1022

💡 Remediation

▼
Add 'noopener,noreferrer' to window.open() calls.

#147 Debug Parameter Accepted A06

medium ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/AJAX/index.php
Parameter: debug
Payload:
debug=true
Evidence:
Response changed when debug parameter added
Description:

The application accepts debug parameter 'debug', potentially exposing sensitive information.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-489

💡 Remediation

▼
Remove or disable debug parameters in production.

#148 Insufficient Attack Signature Detection A09

medium ▼
🔖
A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to detect and respond to attacks.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/AJAX/index.php
Evidence:
Only 0/18 attack signatures triggered blocking (0%)
Description:

The application shows minimal detection and blocking of known attack patterns. Common attack signatures for SQL injection, XSS, command injection, and path traversal were sent without triggering security responses.

Undetected attack types: SQL Injection, XSS

This indicates:
- No WAF or insufficient WAF rules
- Lack of attack detection and logging
- Unable to alert on active attacks in real-time

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-778

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Deploy a Web Application Firewall (WAF) with current rule sets

  2. Implement the OWASP ModSecurity Core Rule Set

  3. Configure logging for all blocked requests

  4. Set up real-time alerting for attack patterns

  5. Integrate with SIEM for correlation and analysis

  6. Regularly update attack signature databases

#151 Session Fixation - Arbitrary Session Accepted Other

medium ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/guestbook.php
Parameter: PHPSESSID
Payload:
FAKEATTACKERSESSIONAAAAAAAAAAA...
Evidence:
Server did not reject arbitrary session ID
Description:

Application may accept arbitrary session identifiers

AI Analysis (Confidence: 75%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A07:2025 - Authentication Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: High - May allow session hijacking

CWE: CWE-384

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Regenerate session ID after successful authentication

  2. Regenerate session ID after privilege level change

  3. Don't accept session IDs from URL parameters

  4. Validate session IDs against expected format

  5. Use secure session management frameworks

  6. Implement session timeout and idle timeout



Example session regeneration:

  • PHP: session_regenerate_id(true)

  • Java: request.getSession().invalidate(); request.getSession(true)

  • .NET: SessionIDManager.CreateSessionID()

  • Python Flask: session.regenerate()

#154 Brute Force - Missing Account Lockout Other

medium ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/userinfo.php
Parameter: login endpoint
Payload:
15 failed attempts for same account
Evidence:
No account lockout detected after multiple failed attempts
Description:

Login endpoint does not implement account lockout after failed attempts

AI Analysis (Confidence: 60%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow credential compromise

CWE: CWE-307

💡 Remediation

▼
Implement account lockout after 5-10 failed attempts
Use progressive delays between attempts
Notify users of suspicious login activity
Provide secure account recovery mechanisms
Example policy:

Lock account for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts

Extend lockout duration with repeated lockouts

Send email notification on lockout

Allow unlock via email verification

add_header Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self'" always;
Apache:
apache
Header always set X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN"
Header always set Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self'"
Express.js:
javascript
const helmet = require('helmet');
app.use(helmet.frameguard({ action: 'sameorigin' }));
``


  1. Avoid JavaScript Frame-Busting



  • JavaScript solutions can be bypassed

  • Use HTTP headers instead

#168 Session Fixation - Arbitrary Session Accepted Other

medium ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/listproducts.php?artist=3
Parameter: PHPSESSID
Payload:
FAKEATTACKERSESSIONAAAAAAAAAAA...
Evidence:
Server did not reject arbitrary session ID
Description:

Application may accept arbitrary session identifiers

AI Analysis (Confidence: 75%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A07:2025 - Authentication Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: High - May allow session hijacking

CWE: CWE-384

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Regenerate session ID after successful authentication

  2. Regenerate session ID after privilege level change

  3. Don't accept session IDs from URL parameters

  4. Validate session IDs against expected format

  5. Use secure session management frameworks

  6. Implement session timeout and idle timeout



Example session regeneration:

  • PHP: session_regenerate_id(true)

  • Java: request.getSession().invalidate(); request.getSession(true)

  • .NET: SessionIDManager.CreateSessionID()

  • Python Flask: session.regenerate()

#169 Missing Clickjacking Protection A06

medium ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/artists.php?artist=1
Parameter: X-Frame-Options
Payload:
N/A
Evidence:
Neither X-Frame-Options nor CSP frame-ancestors present
Description:

The page can be embedded in an iframe, potentially allowing clickjacking attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1021

💡 Remediation

▼
Clickjacking Prevention:


  1. Use X-Frame-Options Header


X-Frame-Options: DENY

or
X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN



  1. Use Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors (Recommended)


Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'self'

or
Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none'



  1. For Specific Trusted Origins



``Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'self' https://trusted.com


    

  1. Server Configuration Examples:
    2. 



Nginx:
nginx
add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
add_header Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self'" always;
Apache:
apache
Header always set X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN"
Header always set Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self'"
Express.js:
javascript
const helmet = require('helmet');
app.use(helmet.frameguard({ action: 'sameorigin' }));
``


  1. Avoid JavaScript Frame-Busting



  • JavaScript solutions can be bypassed

  • Use HTTP headers instead

#170 Window.opener Vulnerability A06

medium ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/artists.php?artist=1
Parameter: JavaScript
Payload:
.open('./comment.php?aid=1'
Evidence:
window.open() without noopener/noreferrer
Description:

window.open() is used without noopener, allowing the opened page to access window.opener.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1022

💡 Remediation

▼
Add 'noopener,noreferrer' to window.open() calls.

Example session regeneration:

  • PHP: session_regenerate_id(true)

  • Java: request.getSession().invalidate(); request.getSession(true)

  • .NET: SessionIDManager.CreateSessionID()

  • Python Flask: session.regenerate()

#189 Missing Clickjacking Protection A06

medium ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/product.php?pic=7
Parameter: X-Frame-Options
Payload:
N/A
Evidence:
Neither X-Frame-Options nor CSP frame-ancestors present
Description:

The page can be embedded in an iframe, potentially allowing clickjacking attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1021

💡 Remediation

▼
Clickjacking Prevention:


  1. Use X-Frame-Options Header


X-Frame-Options: DENY

or
X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN



  1. Use Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors (Recommended)


Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'self'

or
Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'none'



  1. For Specific Trusted Origins



``Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'self' https://trusted.com


    

  1. Server Configuration Examples:
    2. 



Nginx:
nginx
add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
add_header Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self'" always;
Apache:
apache
Header always set X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN"
Header always set Content-Security-Policy "frame-ancestors 'self'"
Express.js:
javascript
const helmet = require('helmet');
app.use(helmet.frameguard({ action: 'sameorigin' }));
``


  1. Avoid JavaScript Frame-Busting



  • JavaScript solutions can be bypassed

  • Use HTTP headers instead

#190 Session Fixation - Arbitrary Session Accepted Other

medium ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/product.php?pic=7
Parameter: PHPSESSID
Payload:
FAKEATTACKERSESSIONAAAAAAAAAAA...
Evidence:
Server did not reject arbitrary session ID
Description:

Application may accept arbitrary session identifiers

AI Analysis (Confidence: 75%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A07:2025 - Authentication Failures

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: High - May allow session hijacking

CWE: CWE-384

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Regenerate session ID after successful authentication

  2. Regenerate session ID after privilege level change

  3. Don't accept session IDs from URL parameters

  4. Validate session IDs against expected format

  5. Use secure session management frameworks

  6. Implement session timeout and idle timeout



Example session regeneration:

  • PHP: session_regenerate_id(true)

  • Java: request.getSession().invalidate(); request.getSession(true)

  • .NET: SessionIDManager.CreateSessionID()

  • Python Flask: session.regenerate()

#194 Window.opener Vulnerability A06

medium ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/listproducts.php?cat=2
Parameter: JavaScript
Payload:
.open('./comment.php?pid=6'
Evidence:
window.open() without noopener/noreferrer
Description:

window.open() is used without noopener, allowing the opened page to access window.opener.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1022

💡 Remediation

▼
Add 'noopener,noreferrer' to window.open() calls.
