#206 Missing Security Header: X-Content-Type-Options A02

low ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Evidence:
Header 'X-Content-Type-Options' is not present in the response
Description:

X-Content-Type-Options header not set. Prevents MIME type sniffing which can lead to XSS attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 60%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A02:2025 - Security Misconfiguration

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Low - Increases attack surface

CWE: CWE-693

💡 Remediation

▼
Add the following header to your server configuration:
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff

#207 Missing Security Header: X-XSS-Protection A02

low ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Evidence:
Header 'X-XSS-Protection' is not present in the response
Description:

X-XSS-Protection header not set. Enables browser XSS filtering (legacy, but still useful for older browsers).

AI Analysis (Confidence: 60%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A02:2025 - Security Misconfiguration

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Low - Increases attack surface

CWE: CWE-79

💡 Remediation

▼
Add the following header to your server configuration:
X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block

#208 Missing Security Header: Referrer-Policy A02

low ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Evidence:
Header 'Referrer-Policy' is not present in the response
Description:

Referrer-Policy header not set. Controls how much referrer information is sent with requests.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 60%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A02:2025 - Security Misconfiguration

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Low - Increases attack surface

CWE: CWE-200

💡 Remediation

▼
Add the following header to your server configuration:
Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin

#209 Missing Security Header: Permissions-Policy A02

low ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Evidence:
Header 'Permissions-Policy' is not present in the response
Description:

Permissions-Policy header not set. Controls which browser features can be used (formerly Feature-Policy).

AI Analysis (Confidence: 80%):
The absence of the 'Permissions-Policy' header in the response indicates a potential security misconfiguration, as it allows for unrestricted access to sensitive features. This could lead to security vulnerabilities if not properly configured. Further testing is required to determine the full extent of this issue.

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: An attacker could potentially exploit this vulnerability to gain unauthorized access to sensitive features, resulting in a significant business impact.

CWE: CWE-693

💡 Remediation

▼
Add the following header to your server configuration:
Permissions-Policy: geolocation=(), microphone=(), camera=()

#210 Missing Security Header: X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies A02

low ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Evidence:
Header 'X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies' is not present in the response
Description:

X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies header not set. Controls cross-domain policies for Flash and PDF.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 60%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A02:2025 - Security Misconfiguration

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Low - Increases attack surface

CWE: CWE-693

💡 Remediation

▼
Add the following header to your server configuration:
X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies: none

Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025

/.idea/workspace.xml

File accessible: IntelliJ workspace

#212 Missing Rate Limiting Other

low ▼
🔖
Other: Other
Vulnerabilities not directly mapped to OWASP Top 10 2025
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Parameter: N/A
Payload:
Sent 20 requests in burst
Evidence:
20/20 requests succeeded without throttling
Description:

Endpoint lacks rate limiting

AI Analysis (Confidence: 65%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow brute force or DoS attacks

CWE: CWE-770

💡 Remediation

▼
Implement rate limiting using:

  1. Token bucket algorithm

  2. Sliding window counters

  3. Fixed window counters


Use libraries like express-rate-limit, Flask-Limiter, or API gateway rate limiting.

#213 Partial Attack Signature Detection A09

low ▼
🔖
A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to detect and respond to attacks.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Evidence:
Detected 6/18 attack signatures (33%)
Description:

The application has some attack detection capabilities but coverage is incomplete. Detection rate: 33%

Some attack types may not trigger alerts, reducing visibility into active attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-778

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Review and expand WAF rule coverage

  2. Add rules for undetected attack patterns

  3. Enable logging for all rule matches, not just blocks

#214 No Scan Detection / Rate Limiting A09

low ▼
🔖
A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to detect and respond to attacks.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Evidence:
Completed 30/30 rapid requests (4.5 req/s) without triggering rate limiting or blocking
Description:

The application allowed rapid successive requests without rate limiting or blocking. This scanning behavior pattern was not detected:
- Sent 30 requests in 6.7 seconds
- Rate: 4.5 requests/second
- No blocking or throttling observed

Attackers can freely scan and enumerate the application without detection. This allows reconnaissance activities to go unnoticed.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 65%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow brute force or DoS attacks

CWE: CWE-778

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement rate limiting on all endpoints

  2. Configure alerting for abnormal request rates

  3. Use tools like fail2ban to detect and block scanning

  4. Implement progressive delays for suspicious patterns

  5. Log and alert on rapid sequential requests from single IPs

#215 Sensitive Path Exposed: IntelliJ Config A01

low ▼
🔖
A01: Broken Access Control
Failures in access control enforcement, allowing users to act outside intended permissions. Now includes SSRF.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/.idea/
Evidence:
HTTP 200 - IntelliJ Config accessible at .idea/
Description:

The sensitive resource '.idea/' is publicly accessible. This could expose sensitive information or functionality.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-425

💡 Remediation

▼
Restrict access to sensitive paths using proper authentication and authorization. Consider removing unnecessary files from production servers.

#229 Unsafe External Links (Tabnabbing) A06

low ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/disclaimer.php
Parameter: HTML Links
Payload:
Found 1 link(s) with target='_blank' without rel='noopener'
Evidence:
1 vulnerable link(s)
Description:

Links with target='_blank' without rel='noopener noreferrer' can lead to reverse tabnabbing attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1022

💡 Remediation

▼
Add rel='noopener noreferrer' to all external links with target='_blank'.

#234 No Attack Detection Observed A09

low ▼
🔖
A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to detect and respond to attacks.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/privacy.php
Evidence:
Sent 6 malicious payloads without triggering any visible security response
Description:

The application did not show any visible indication of detecting or blocking obviously malicious input (SQL injection, XSS, path traversal, etc.). While this doesn't confirm vulnerabilities exist, it suggests:
- No Web Application Firewall (WAF) or it's not properly configured
- Insufficient logging of suspicious input patterns
- Lack of real-time attack detection and alerting

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-778

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement input validation logging for suspicious patterns

  2. Consider deploying a Web Application Firewall (WAF)

  3. Set up alerting for common attack signatures

  4. Integrate with SIEM for centralized security monitoring

  5. Implement the OWASP ModSecurity Core Rule Set

The application did not show any visible indication of detecting or blocking obviously malicious input (SQL injection, XSS, path traversal, etc.). While this doesn't confirm vulnerabilities exist, it suggests:
- No Web Application Firewall (WAF) or it's not properly configured
- Insufficient logging of suspicious input patterns
- Lack of real-time attack detection and alerting

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-778

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement input validation logging for suspicious patterns

  2. Consider deploying a Web Application Firewall (WAF)

  3. Set up alerting for common attack signatures

  4. Integrate with SIEM for centralized security monitoring

  5. Implement the OWASP ModSecurity Core Rule Set

  4. Integrate with SIEM for centralized security monitoring

  5. Implement the OWASP ModSecurity Core Rule Set

#270 No Scan Detection / Rate Limiting A09

low ▼
🔖
A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to detect and respond to attacks.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/AJAX/index.php
Evidence:
Completed 30/30 rapid requests (4.6 req/s) without triggering rate limiting or blocking
Description:

The application allowed rapid successive requests without rate limiting or blocking. This scanning behavior pattern was not detected:
- Sent 30 requests in 6.5 seconds
- Rate: 4.6 requests/second
- No blocking or throttling observed

Attackers can freely scan and enumerate the application without detection. This allows reconnaissance activities to go unnoticed.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 65%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: A06:2025 - Insecure Design

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: Medium - May allow brute force or DoS attacks

CWE: CWE-778

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Implement rate limiting on all endpoints

  2. Configure alerting for abnormal request rates

  3. Use tools like fail2ban to detect and block scanning

  4. Implement progressive delays for suspicious patterns

  5. Log and alert on rapid sequential requests from single IPs

#271 Missing Content Security Policy A08

low ▼
🔖
A08: Software or Data Integrity Failures
Code and infrastructure without integrity verification, insecure deserialization, unsigned updates.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/guestbook.php
Parameter: HTTP Headers
Payload:
N/A
Evidence:
No Content-Security-Policy header
Description:

No CSP header to enforce script integrity requirements.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-353

💡 Remediation

▼
Implement Content-Security-Policy with strict-dynamic or hash-based policies.

#288 Unsafe External Links (Tabnabbing) A06

low ▼
🔖
A06: Insecure Design
Missing or ineffective security controls, architectural flaws, business logic vulnerabilities.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/listproducts.php?artist=1
Parameter: HTML Links
Payload:
Found 6 link(s) with target='_blank' without rel='noopener'
Evidence:
6 vulnerable link(s)
Description:

Links with target='_blank' without rel='noopener noreferrer' can lead to reverse tabnabbing attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1022

💡 Remediation

▼
Add rel='noopener noreferrer' to all external links with target='_blank'.

URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/listproducts.php?cat=2
Parameter: HTML Links
Payload:
Found 1 link(s) with target='_blank' without rel='noopener'
Evidence:
1 vulnerable link(s)
Description:

Links with target='_blank' without rel='noopener noreferrer' can lead to reverse tabnabbing attacks.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-1022

💡 Remediation

▼
Add rel='noopener noreferrer' to all external links with target='_blank'.

#305 Missing Content Security Policy A08

low ▼
🔖
A08: Software or Data Integrity Failures
Code and infrastructure without integrity verification, insecure deserialization, unsigned updates.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/listproducts.php?cat=2
Parameter: HTTP Headers
Payload:
N/A
Evidence:
No Content-Security-Policy header
Description:

No CSP header to enforce script integrity requirements.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-353

💡 Remediation

▼
Implement Content-Security-Policy with strict-dynamic or hash-based policies.

#313 Information Disclosure: Server A02

info ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Evidence:
Server: nginx/1.19.0
Description:

The server reveals technology information via the Server header. This information can help attackers identify vulnerabilities specific to the technology stack.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 80%):
The server is disclosing its version, which could be used by attackers to identify potential vulnerabilities. The presence of PHP and MySQL in the tech stack increases the attack surface. However, without further information, the impact is limited to information disclosure.

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: An attacker could use the disclosed server version to plan a targeted attack, potentially leading to unauthorized access or data breaches.

CWE: CWE-200

💡 Remediation

▼
Remove or suppress the Server header in your server configuration.

#314 Information Disclosure: X-Powered-By A02

info ▼
🔖
A02: Security Misconfiguration
Missing security hardening, default credentials, overly permissive cloud settings, verbose errors.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Evidence:
X-Powered-By: PHP/5.6.40-38+ubuntu20.04.1+deb.sury.org+1
Description:

The server reveals technology information via the X-Powered-By header. This information can help attackers identify vulnerabilities specific to the technology stack.

AI Analysis (Confidence: 80%):
The X-Powered-By header reveals the PHP version, which could be used to identify potential vulnerabilities, but it does not directly expose sensitive data. The tech stack includes PHP and MySQL, which may have known vulnerabilities. However, this information disclosure is more related to security misconfiguration than a direct vulnerability.

Exploitation Complexity: Low
Business Impact: The business impact is relatively low as this information disclosure does not directly expose sensitive data, but it could aid in further vulnerability exploitation.

CWE: CWE-200

💡 Remediation

▼
Remove or suppress the X-Powered-By header in your server configuration.

#315 Missing Request Correlation Headers A09

info ▼
🔖
A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to detect and respond to attacks.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com
Evidence:
No X-Request-ID or similar correlation headers found in response
Description:

The application does not return request correlation headers (X-Request-ID, X-Correlation-ID, etc.). While not a vulnerability itself, these headers are a best practice that:
- Enable request tracing across distributed systems
- Help correlate logs for incident investigation
- Aid in debugging and performance monitoring

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-778

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Generate unique request IDs for each request

  2. Include request ID in all log entries

  3. Return request ID in response headers

  4. Propagate IDs across microservices

  4. Propagate IDs across microservices

#335 Missing Request Correlation Headers A09

info ▼
🔖
A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting. Failure to detect and respond to attacks.
URL: http://testphp.vulnweb.com/userinfo.php
Evidence:
No X-Request-ID or similar correlation headers found in response
Description:

The application does not return request correlation headers (X-Request-ID, X-Correlation-ID, etc.). While not a vulnerability itself, these headers are a best practice that:
- Enable request tracing across distributed systems
- Help correlate logs for incident investigation
- Aid in debugging and performance monitoring

AI Analysis (Confidence: 70%):
Heuristic-based analysis (non-AI mode). OWASP 2025: Unclassified

Exploitation Complexity: Medium
Business Impact: Requires manual verification

CWE: CWE-778

💡 Remediation

▼

  1. Generate unique request IDs for each request

  2. Include request ID in all log entries

  3. Return request ID in response headers

  4. Propagate IDs across microservices
